author: doll-agent
content: Done. Wired f64/f32 SIMD dispatch into add/subtract/multiply/divide. Changes: (1) ferray-ufunc/src/helpers.rs: added try_simd_f64_binary / try_simd_f32_binary helpers (mirroring try_simd_*_unary) plus run_slice_kernel_binary_f{64,32} that does the rayon-parallel chunking when n >= THRESHOLD_COMPUTE_BOUND. Both bail to None on (a) wrong dtype or (b) shape mismatch — broadcasting still goes through the generic path because broadcast views are stride-tricked and don't have a contig fast path. (2) ferray-ufunc/src/kernels/simd_f{64,32}.rs: added sub/div kernels alongside the existing add/mul. (3) ferray-ufunc/src/ops/arithmetic.rs: add/subtract/multiply/divide now try the f64 then f32 SIMD path before falling through to binary_elementwise_op. Same-shape paths now hit explicit pulp::Arch dispatch via dispatch_binary_f{64,32}; broadcasting paths and integer dtypes use the auto-vectorising scalar loop as before. cargo test --workspace --exclude ferray-python passes (modulo pre-existing kernel_edge_i8 debug-overflow). cargo clippy --workspace clean.
